identity.name: Mass ESXi VM Encryption Orchestrated via Ansible (BlackSuit Pattern)
identity.summary: An attacker with SSH access to multiple VMware ESXi hosts uses Ansible (or a custom shell loop) to: (1) terminate all running VMs via `vim-cmd vmsvc/power.off`, (2) drop a Linux/ESXi ransomware binary, (3) encrypt VMDK files in parallel across the entire estate. In the BlackSuit Blitz incident, ~60 ESXi hosts were encrypted in a single coordinated wave.
identity.description: ESXi is the highest-impact target in modern ransomware operations — a single host encrypts dozens of VMs, and most organizations under-protect hypervisors with traditional EDR. Unit 42's BlackSuit Blitz report describes Ansible-orchestrated encryption across ~60 ESXi hosts after credential theft (TTP-2026-0004) and data exfil (TTP-2026-0005). This TTP requires Cortex XDR for Linux on ESXi (a comparatively new capability) plus XSIAM correlation across vCenter audit logs, ESXi /var/log, SSH session records, and network flow from a single jumphost spraying across the hypervisor cluster. The destructive nature of real encryption means the lab simulation creates `.tttt` marker files instead of actually encrypting VMDKs.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell

prerequisites.description: Lab vSphere cluster: ≥3 ESXi hosts, each with ≥2 powered-on lab VMs. Test VMs should be snapshot-protected.
Attacker-controlled jumphost (Linux) with SSH key authorized on all lab ESXi hosts.

payload.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Build Ansible inventory of lab ESXi hosts.
cat > /tmp/pov-esxi-hosts <<EOF
[esxi]
${ESXI_HOST_LIST}

[esxi:vars]
ansible_user=root
ansible_ssh_private_key_file=${SSH_KEY_PATH}
ansible_python_interpreter=/bin/python
ansible_ssh_common_args='-o StrictHostKeyChecking=no'
EOF

# Build the playbook — SAFE-MODE: drop marker file instead of encrypting.
cat > /tmp/pov-encrypt.yml <<'EOF'
---
- name: POV-SAFE mass-encryption simulation
  hosts: esxi
  gather_facts: false
  tasks:

    - name: Enumerate running VMs
      shell: vim-cmd vmsvc/getallvms | awk 'NR>1 {print $1}'
      register: vm_ids

    - name: Power off running VMs (lab — they will be powered back on by cleanup)
      shell: "vim-cmd vmsvc/power.off {{ item }} || true"
      loop: "{{ vm_ids.stdout_lines }}"

    - name: Drop ransomware marker (NOT actual encryption)
      copy:
        dest: "/vmfs/volumes/datastore1/POV-RUN-{{ pov_run_id }}-readme.txt"
        content: |
          POV simulation run {{ pov_run_id }} on {{ inventory_hostname }} at {{ ansible_date_time.iso8601 }}
          (This file is a stand-in for a ransom note. No VMDKs were encrypted.)

    - name: Touch marker files alongside each VMDK (encryption simulator)
      shell: |
        find /vmfs/volumes/ -maxdepth 4 -name '*.vmdk' -print0 | \
          xargs -0 -I{} touch "{}.tttt"
EOF

# Run the playbook in parallel across all ESXi hosts.
ansible-playbook -i /tmp/pov-esxi-hosts -e pov_run_id=${POV_RUN_ID} /tmp/pov-encrypt.yml -f 16

input_variables.name: ESXI_HOST_LIST
input_variables.description: Newline-delimited list of lab ESXi host FQDNs.

input_variables.name: SSH_KEY_PATH
input_variables.description: Path to SSH private key authorized on lab ESXi root.

expected_artifacts.description: vim-cmd processes spawned in burst across many ESXi hosts within seconds of each other.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: process_name = 'vim-cmd' AND command_line CONTAINS 'power.off' AND host_count > 3 within 5m

expected_artifacts.description: Marker files (.tttt) or ransom notes appearing on /vmfs/volumes/ datastores across many hosts.
SSH sessions from a single source IP to ≥3 ESXi hosts within 5 minutes.
Ansible process pattern on the jumphost (ansible-playbook with -f >= 8).

cleanup.code:
# Remove marker files; power VMs back on.
cat > /tmp/pov-cleanup.yml <<'EOF'
---
- name: POV cleanup
  hosts: esxi
  gather_facts: false
  tasks:
    - name: Remove marker files
      shell: find /vmfs/volumes/ -maxdepth 4 -name '*.tttt' -delete
    - name: Remove readme files
      shell: find /vmfs/volumes/ -maxdepth 4 -name 'POV-RUN-*-readme.txt' -delete
    - name: List VMs
      shell: vim-cmd vmsvc/getallvms | awk 'NR>1 {print $1}'
      register: vmids
    - name: Power on VMs
      shell: "vim-cmd vmsvc/power.on {{ item }} || true"
      loop: "{{ vmids.stdout_lines }}"
EOF
ansible-playbook -i /tmp/pov-esxi-hosts /tmp/pov-cleanup.yml -f 16
manual_steps: Confirm all lab VMs returned to Powered On state.
Rotate the ESXi root SSH key after each POV run.
Validate snapshots are intact in case rollback is needed.

iocs.context: Lab marker — production indicator is the actual ransomware extension which varies (.blacksuit, .royal, .akira, etc.).

biocs.name: ESXi SSH Logon Burst — Single Source → Many Hypervisors
biocs.description: ≥3 ESXi hosts receive successful SSH logon from the same source IP within 5 minutes.
biocs.logic:
dataset = esxi_syslog
| filter event_source = "sshd" and event_message contains "Accepted"
| bin _time span=5m
| comp count_distinct(hostname) as host_count by source_ip, _time
| filter host_count >= 3

biocs.name: vim-cmd Mass Power-Off Across ESXi Cluster
biocs.description: vim-cmd power.off invoked across multiple ESXi hosts within a short window — a strong precursor to mass encryption.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter agent_os_type = ENUM.AGENT_OS_LINUX
| filter action_process_image_name = "vim-cmd" and action_process_image_command_line contains "power.off"
| bin _time span=5m
| comp count_distinct(agent_hostname) as h by _time
| filter h >= 3

biocs.name: Mass File Creation Adjacent to VMDK Files
biocs.description: ≥10 files created on /vmfs/volumes/ with the same non-VMDK extension within 5 minutes — the encryption-marker signature.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and event_sub_type = ENUM.FILE_CREATE
| filter action_file_path starts_with "/vmfs/volumes/"
| filter action_file_extension not_in ("vmdk","vmx","vmsd","vmsn","log","nvram")
| bin _time span=5m
| comp count() as creates by action_file_extension, agent_hostname, _time
| filter creates >= 10

biocs.name: Ansible-Pattern Process on Jumphost With ESXi Reach
biocs.description: ansible-playbook process invoked on a host that subsequently opens SSH to multiple ESXi targets.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter action_process_image_name = "ansible-playbook"
| join (
    preset = xdr_data | filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and dst_port = 22 and dst_host in (vcenter.esxi_hosts)
  ) on agent_hostname within 10m

xql_queries.name: Confirm mass vim-cmd power.off across cluster
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter agent_os_type = ENUM.AGENT_OS_LINUX
| filter action_process_image_name = "vim-cmd" and action_process_image_command_line contains "power.off"
| comp count() by agent_hostname
| sort count desc

xql_queries.name: Confirm SSH logon burst at ESXi syslog layer
xql_queries.query:
dataset = esxi_syslog
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_source = "sshd" and event_message contains "Accepted"
| comp count_distinct(hostname) as hosts by source_ip
| filter hosts >= 3

xql_queries.name: Confirm marker files dropped on datastores
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and event_sub_type = ENUM.FILE_CREATE
| filter action_file_path starts_with "/vmfs/volumes/"
| filter action_file_extension = "tttt"
| comp count() by agent_hostname

xql_queries.name: ESXi hosts with SSH from unusual source ASNs in last 7d
xql_queries.query:
dataset = esxi_syslog
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 604800)
| filter event_source = "sshd" and event_message contains "Accepted"
| enrich asn = geoip(source_ip).asn
| filter asn not in corp_baseline.esxi_admin_asns
| comp count() by source_ip, hostname

correlation_rules.name: Ransomware — ESXi Mass Encryption Sequence
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(ESXi SSH Logon Burst — Single Source → Many Hypervisors) AND BIOC(vim-cmd Mass Power-Off Across ESXi Cluster) AND BIOC(Mass File Creation Adjacent to VMDK Files) within 15m

correlation_rules.name: Ransomware — Full Kill Chain (Identity → Exfil → ESXi Encrypt)
correlation_rules.logic: CR-CRED-0003 (LSASS + DCSync) AND CR-EXFIL-0001 (rclone exfil) AND CR-RANSOM-0001 (ESXi mass encrypt) within 7d on related identities/hosts — auto-promotes to P1 incident.

analytics_modules: Cortex XDR for Linux (ESXi) — destructive activity
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — anomalous SSH fan-out
Cortex XSIAM Cross-Source Correlation — vCenter + ESXi syslog + XDR

products.evidence_query: Datasets: esxi_syslog, vcenter_audit, panw.endpoint (linux). Confirm all three are flowing during POV.
Playbook: ESXi Containment — disable SSH on affected hosts via vCenter API, snapshot volatile state, isolate management network.
Segmentation rule restricting SSH/HTTPS to ESXi management subnets — should deny lateral SSH from non-admin hosts.

use_cases.name: Hypervisor-Tier Ransomware Defense
use_cases.description: Detect and contain mass ESXi encryption — the modern ransomware end-stage — by chaining XDR-for-Linux telemetry with vCenter and ESXi syslog correlation in XSIAM.

test_cases.objective: Cortex XDR for Linux raises alerts on the vim-cmd mass power-off and the marker-file creation burst within 2 minutes.
preconditions: Cortex XDR for Linux agent installed on lab ESXi hosts (supported variant) or VIB-equivalent telemetry forwarding to XSIAM.
steps: Run TTP-2026-0006 payload in SAFE mode.
Observe alerts on jumphost, ESXi hosts, and XSIAM.
success_criteria: BIOC `vim-cmd Mass Power-Off Across ESXi Cluster` fires within 2m.
BIOC `Mass File Creation Adjacent to VMDK Files` fires within 2m.
Both alerts attached to the same XSIAM incident.

test_cases.objective: Cross-source correlation in XSIAM links the jumphost Ansible activity to the ESXi outcome.
success_criteria: BIOC `Ansible-Pattern Process on Jumphost With ESXi Reach` fires.
Single incident contains: ansible-playbook process, SSH session burst, vim-cmd activity, marker creation.

test_cases.objective: Full-chain correlation against prior TTPs (`CR-RANSOM-0002`) elevates to P1.
preconditions: TTP-2026-0001 (help-desk MFA reset) executed earlier in the run.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-RANSOM-0002` fires, linking all four prior incidents.
War Room timeline shows the full kill chain end-to-end.

test_cases.objective: XSOAR containment disables SSH at vCenter and isolates affected ESXi hosts.
success_criteria: vCenter API call disables SSH service on affected hosts within 3 minutes.
Management-network segmentation rule pushed to NGFW.

preventive_controls: Disable ESXi SSH by default; enable only on demand with time-boxed approvals.
Strict NGFW segmentation of ESXi management network — admin jumpbox subnet only.
Lockdown Mode for ESXi hosts; manage exclusively through vCenter.
Immutable / offline backups for VM datastores; verify restore quarterly.
MFA on vCenter; rotate ESXi root credentials regularly.
detection_engineering: Ingest esxi_syslog + vcenter_audit into XSIAM as first-class datasets.
Baseline SSH session counts to ESXi management subnets; alert on fan-out anomalies.
Hunt for vim-cmd power.off bursts and find-on-/vmfs/volumes file-creation bursts.
Build correlation rules that fuse XDR-Linux process telemetry with ESXi syslog and vCenter task events.

references.title: Anatomy of an Attack: The 'BlackSuit Blitz' at a Global Equipment Manufacturer
MITRE ATT&CK T1486 — Data Encrypted for Impact
MITRE ATT&CK T1490 — Inhibit System Recovery
